description: TrustyBits Inc. parent organization - team collaboration, enterprise workflows, integration registry, and AI orchestration framework

do: Create sandbox projects for exploration before implementation
Follow proper 8825 mining workflow (Stage 1: Report, Stage 2: Extract)
Test fundamental assumptions before designing solutions
Use breadth-first validation for team capabilities
Leverage existing cloud provider sync mechanisms
Conduct comprehensive 4-week discovery process before onboarding recommendations
Gather strategic context, usage patterns, workflow integration, and technical infrastructure data
Create scenario-based implementation paths (A/B/C/D) based on team profile
Build starter kits with setup wizards for streamlined new user onboarding
Develop platform-specific extraction tools (ChatGPT, Claude, Windsurf)
Use phased rollout approach matching organizational readiness levels
Establish clear success metrics and evaluation frameworks before implementation
Position Joju as proof-of-work identity/reputation layer
Quantify value across conservative/realistic/optimistic cases (to be developed)
dont: Jump to implementation without understanding current capabilities
Design complex multi-workspace solutions before validation
Skip the mining report generation process
Over-engineer team workflows before proving core value
Make assumptions about team LLM usage patterns without data collection
Create one-size-fits-all onboarding without assessing team technical comfort
Build tools before understanding actual workflow integration points
Do not overstate valuations without milestones
Do not separate value thesis from mission ethics
framing: Team workflows as extension of PMCE infrastructure
Workspace isolation as constraint to work within, not around
Enterprise features come after core value validation with 10-20 users
Onboarding success depends on thorough discovery and customized implementation paths
Team adoption requires matching technical solutions to actual usage patterns and comfort levels
Discovery investment pays dividends in adoption success and long-term value creation
Unified operating system for human + AI collaboration
Anti-algorithm stance: user consent and control

lexicon.definition: Each Windsurf workspace operates independently with its own Cascade chat history and file context
examples: /8825 workspace for knowledge management
/joju workspace for product development
Question: Can Cascade access files across workspaces?

lexicon.definition: Separation between meta-system (8825 knowledge management) and product development (Joju codebase)
examples: Workspace 1: 8825 library with projects/*.json
Workspace 2: Joju development with src/components
Challenge: How to bridge Cascade conversations between them

lexicon.definition: Potential capability for Cascade to analyze its own conversation history and generate mining reports
examples: Developer asks: 'Generate mining report from today's conversation'
Cascade outputs markdown following mining report template
Status: Unknown if Cascade can access its own history

lexicon.definition: Manual workflow where team generates mining reports and saves to shared location for extraction
examples: Developer in /joju workspace → Cascade conversation → Manual mining report → Save to shared/- REF -/
8825 system extracts from shared location → Updates projects/*.json
Pros: Works with workspace isolation, Cons: Manual trigger required

lexicon.definition: Systematic approach to validate Windsurf/Cascade capabilities before architecture design
examples: Test 1: Can Cascade access own conversation history?
Test 2: Can Cascade read files across workspaces?
Test 3: Where are Cascade chats stored in file system?
Test 4: Can Cascade generate mining reports from sessions?

lexicon.definition: Multi-layer system where individual knowledge bases (private) coexist with shared team knowledge (collaborative) through selective sync patterns
examples: Individual: Personal projects, private insights, draft patterns in ~/8825_personal/
Team: Shared patterns, validated insights, collaborative projects in /team_8825/
Sync: Individual chooses what to promote to team knowledge base
Privacy: Team cannot access individual knowledge without explicit sharing

lexicon.definition: Process for moving insights from individual private knowledge to shared team knowledge base
examples: Individual validates pattern in personal workspace
Marks pattern for team sharing with confidence level
Team review process before integration into shared knowledge
Bidirectional: Team patterns can be forked to individual workspace

lexicon.definition: Clear separation between individual private knowledge and team collaborative knowledge with explicit controls
examples: Individual workspace: Full privacy, no team access
Shared workspace: Team read/write with proper attribution
Promotion queue: Individual controls what moves from private to shared
Audit trail: Track knowledge flow between individual and team layers

lexicon.definition: C-corp incorporated July 8, 2025, parent organization for Joju.app and 76Forge platforms
examples: President: Matthew Galley, Secretary/Treasurer: Cameron Watkins
Mission: Improve peoples capacity and knowledge allowing them qualify for a better quality of life
Vision: Provide alternative to traditional education with measurable trust-building methods
Registered address: 1366 S 1150 W Orem, UT 84058

lexicon.definition: Help tech professionals level up through real-world experience, shared knowledge, and supportive community
examples: Core products: Joju.app (AI-enabled SaaS achievement platform), 76Forge (project platform for skill-building)
Supplementary services: Training, coaching, mentoring, consulting
Revenue streams: SaaS subscriptions, consulting, partnerships & programs
Target customers: Designers, developers, product managers, high school students

lexicon.definition: TrustyBits regulatory compliance approach for AI-enabled features across platforms
examples: Regulations: GDPR, CCPA, COPPA, ADA, FERPA, SOX, PCI compliance
Data practices: User-controlled visibility, detailed privacy policy, strong encryption
AI usage: OpenAI API for resume parsing, in-house system prompts, user opt-in training data
Security: OWASP audits, pen testing bounties, sandbox environment for input review

lexicon.definition: Catalog of integration capabilities, status, and upkeep rules for 8825 ecosystem with readiness scoring and risk assessment
examples: Readiness levels: 0=Exploratory, 1=Pilot, 2=Operational, 3=Hardened
Risk levels: L=Low, M=Medium, H=High
Weekly check cadence with automated monitoring
Tracks auth status, webhook events, SDK versions, playbooks

lexicon.definition: AI orchestration framework defining core archetypes for model role assignment in 8825 ecosystem
examples: Instructor (ChatGPT): Synthesizer for process design, frameworks, storytelling
Analyst (Perplexity): Researcher for market analysis, data validation
Provocateur (Grok): Commentator for tone testing, creative critique, viral phrasing
Planner (Gemini): System integrator for timeline planning, file structuring
Dynamic routing: intent='analyze' → Analyst; intent='explain' → Instructor

lexicon.definition: Modular library architecture for 8825 balancing context, scalability, and standalone deployment capabilities
examples: Registry: Central index listing all libraries, dependencies, versions
8825_core: Canonical frameworks, schemas, shared logic (PromptGen 1.0)
Modular structure for context balance and scalability
Standalone deployment capabilities per library

lexicon.definition: Automated flow for continuously tracking integration capabilities, auth states, and readiness with weekly digests and incident alerts
examples: System roles: Researcher, Validator, Updater, Notifier
Cadence: Weekly research (Mon 9:00 CT), nightly checks (02:00 CT), instant alerts
Outputs: Updated registry, weekly digest, incident alerts, backups
Governance: Single source of truth with versioned archives

summary_log.topic: Initial exploration: Team workflow for Joju development with PMCE integration
summary_log.notes: User has 8825 workspace set up for knowledge management. Joju development team would work in separate workspace. Key question: How do Cascade conversations in Joju workspace get mined into 8825 knowledge base? Discovered workspace isolation as potential constraint. Need to test: (1) Can Cascade access own history? (2) Can Cascade in one workspace read files from another? (3) What's the bridge mechanism between workspaces?

summary_log.topic: Agent capabilities extraction from compressed WCB mining report
summary_log.notes: Successfully tested new compressed mining report format from ChatGPT. Extracted 5 agent capabilities using 6-element WCB compression schema: system_role, inputs_required, outputs, workflow, constraints, routing. Agents cover Joju problem definition, Windsurf-Notion integration, workshop roadmaps, and Reddit beta testing workflows. Validates that external LLMs can generate lightweight, structured reports without verbose text blocks while maintaining full reconstruction capability.

summary_log.topic: Agent capabilities migrated to universal registry
summary_log.notes: Moved 5 agent capabilities to projects/agent_registry.json to eliminate redundancy. Enterprise project now maintains references only. Registry serves as single source of truth for all WCB reconstruction. Establishes pattern for future mining reports: content routes to named projects, agents route to registry.

summary_log.topic: TrustyBits Inc. corporate structure and business model documentation
summary_log.notes: Documented TrustyBits Inc. as parent organization (8825_enterprise_76): C-corp incorporated July 8, 2025, with Matthew Galley (President) and Cameron Watkins (Secretary/Treasurer). Core business model: help tech professionals level up through real-world experience, shared knowledge, and supportive community. Product ecosystem includes Joju.app (AI-enabled SaaS achievement platform) and 76Forge (project platform for skill-building). Revenue streams: SaaS subscriptions, consulting, partnerships. Pre-revenue bootstrapped status with future funding consideration. AI compliance framework includes GDPR, CCPA, COPPA, ADA, FERPA compliance with user-controlled data visibility and strong security standards.

questions: Can Cascade access its own conversation history within a workspace?
Can Cascade generate mining reports from past conversations?
Can Cascade in workspace A read files from workspace B?
Are Cascade conversations workspace-specific or global?
Where does Windsurf store Cascade chat history? (~/.windsurf/? workspace/.windsurf/?)
Does Joju team have existing Windsurf workspace?
Is it in Dropbox or separate location?

options: Option A: Cascade self-mining (if capability exists)
Option B: Manual report generation by developers
Option C: Automated export from Cascade history

tests: Test if Cascade in one can access files from the other
Ask Cascade about conversation history storage
Check file system for Cascade chat storage locations

potential_architectures.name: Shared Dropbox Bridge
potential_architectures.description: Both workspaces point to shared Dropbox folder for knowledge exchange
workflow: Joju workspace: Developer works, chats with Cascade
Save report to shared Dropbox/8825/- REF -/
Updated knowledge syncs back to team

potential_architectures.name: Single Unified Workspace
potential_architectures.description: Combine 8825 knowledge system and Joju code in one workspace
cons: May not match user's mental model

potential_architectures.name: Cascade History Export
potential_architectures.description: Automated script reads Cascade chat files and processes them
workflow: Cascade stores chats in accessible location
Python script monitors Joju workspace chat history

potential_architectures.name: Notion Integration Hub
potential_architectures.description: Lens model sync from 8825 JSON to Notion for team visibility and light curation
workflow: 8825 workspace: Projects stored in JSON (source of truth)
Python sync job: Read projects/*.json → upsert to Notion DBs
Notion: Projects + Open Loops DBs with saved views by client/priority
Team: Browse, comment, light status updates in Notion
